item.title: Vanguard’s VOO Hits $1 Trillion of Assets in ETF Industry First
item.description: A seemingly endless appetite for buying US stock dips has propelled Vanguard Group’s S&P 500-tracking ETF past $1 trillion in assets, making it the first fund of its kind to reach a milestone once thought unimaginable for the ETF industry.

item.title: Dalio Sees AI Bubble Bursting as Wealth Is Converted Into Money
item.description: Billionaire investor Ray Dalio said the booming artificial-intelligence market shows signs of a bubble that will eventually burst.

item.title: Ares Owed $547 Million After Collapse of Textor’s Eagle Football
item.description: Ares Management Corp. was owed more than $547 million following the collapse of John Textor’s Eagle Football Group, according to a filing by the administrators on Wednesday.

item.title: Polish Rates Are ‘High Enough’ as Glapinski Cools Rate Hike Bets
item.description: Poland’s central bank views current interest rate levels as “high enough” to protect price stability amid a spike in global energy costs that’s gradually feeding domestic inflation, Governor Adam Glapinski said.

item.title: Inotiv, Breeder of Laboratory Animals, Files For Bankruptcy
item.description: Inotiv Inc., which breeds dogs and imports monkeys that are used in drug experiments, filed for bankruptcy in part on what it blamed are tighter rules on its US canine farm and tariffs on non-human primates raised in China.

item.title: Musk’s xAI Pauses Hiring for Specialists to Train Grok Chatbot
item.description: Elon Musk’s xAI has paused hiring for professionals to train its Grok chatbot on a range of specialized skills, according to people with knowledge of the matter, marking a potential shift in how the artificial intelligence firm develops its technology.

item.title: Credit Titans Warn of Shakeout on Deals That ‘Don’t Make Sense’
item.description: Beneath the surface of buoyant credit markets, a shakeout is building for leveraged deals made years ago, as well as for direct-lending funds that are grappling with an extended period of investor withdrawals.

item.title: Adobe Eyes Two Internal Leaders, AI Outsiders for CEO Role
item.description: Adobe Inc. is eyeing two internal leaders in its quest for a new chief executive officer and has hired a well-known search firm to find external candidates who could be suited to running the company in the AI era.

item.title: NatWest Names Roose Interim Head of Primary Capital Markets
item.description: NatWest Group Plc has appointed Scott Roose as interim head of its primary capital markets business in a leadership change at the unit.

item.title: Chile Senate Takes Up Kast’s Mega Economic Bill as Warnings Grow
item.description: Chile President José Antonio Kast’s team steamrolled his centerpiece economic bill through the lower house of Congress last month. Now it’s the turn of the Senate, and the dynamic is very different.

item.title: Fed’s Williams Says No Obvious Direction Ahead for Rates
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said US monetary policy is well positioned for now but he sees no obvious direction for the future path of interest rates.

item.title: ASML Becomes Europe’s Most Valuable Stock Ever on AI Boom
item.description: ASML Holding NV became Europe’s most valuable company ever after a 60% year-to-date advance fueled by a clamor for stocks with exposure to the buildout of artificial intelligence data centers.

item.title: Private Credit Marks Drawing More Scrutiny From SDNY Prosecutors
item.description: Wall Street’s top prosecutor said his office is looking at possible valuation discrepancies in the private credit marketplace.

item.title: Private Credit Stocks Fall After Cliffwater Redemption Requests
item.description: Shares of the biggest alternative asset managers tumbled Wednesday after Cliffwater LLC’s flagship private credit fund reported even larger redemption requests than in the first quarter, setting off a new wave of fears about the industry.

item.title: Brookfield Bets on AI at Scale Never Tested Before in $50 Billion Push
item.description: Bloom Energy Corp. struggled for years to convince investors its fuel cells were a practical alternative to cheaper sources of electricity. Then Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. came along right as the AI boom ramped up.

item.title: Waller’s Push to Shake Up Fed Stirs Fears Over Regional Autonomy
item.description: A proposal to streamline Federal Reserve operations has raised alarm inside the sprawling institution over whether it would undermine the autonomy of its 12 regional banks, just as the central bank braces for broader changes from its new chairman, Kevin Warsh.

item.title: Alphabet Upsizes Equity Offering to $85 Billion for AI Spending
item.description: Google parent Alphabet Inc. upsized its equity raise to $84.75 billion from the $80 billion it announced just two days earlier in a bid to help fund growing artificial intelligence spending plans.

item.title: Uber Cuts 23% of People Division as New President Takes Over
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. said it is cutting 23% of jobs in a division that includes human resources, recruitment, workplace facilities and culture, part of a move by the rideshare company’s newly promoted president Jill Hazelbaker to simplify team structure.

item.title: Russia’s Multi-Billion Payouts to Refiners Dent State Revenues
item.description: Russia’s payouts to refiners in May were near the highest in more than two years, denting the nation’s oil and gas revenues even as the war in Iran helped to boost crude prices.

item.title: A 180% Crypto Rally Shows New Investing Era as Bitcoin Stumbles
item.description: As billions of dollars leave Bitcoin and Ether funds, money is flowing into a corner of crypto that promises something investors have long struggled to find in digital assets: a clearer path from economic activity to token value.

item.title: Meta Sells AI Agent for Businesses in Push to Monetize Service
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is selling businesses access to an artificial intelligence agent for the first time, its latest effort to generate revenue to offset the company’s hefty AI investments.

item.title: Canada Labor Productivity Falls as Construction, Agriculture Lag Behind
item.description: Canada’s labor productivity declined for a second consecutive quarter as hours worked increased while output declined.

item.title: Kenya to Press Ahead With US Ebola Center, Health Secretary Says
item.description: Kenya will continue work on an isolation and treatment unit at an airbase in the East African nation that can be used to house US servicemen exposed to the Ebola virus, its health secretary said.

item.title: Vedanta Unit Targets IPO in New York to Fund Zambia Copper Mines
item.description: A Vedanta Resources Ltd. unit has applied to sell its shares in New York as part of efforts to raise money for the Zambian copper mining complex it owns.

item.title: Zambia Set for Talks With Bondholders Blocking Its Debt Buyback
item.description: Zambia’s government is heading to the negotiating table after a group of bondholders blocked its attempt to buy back a $1.36 billion bond before a potentially costly coupon increase.

item.title: Bank of America Bucks AI Job Fears With 2,000 Summer Interns
item.description: Bank of America Corp. is continuing to hire even as artificial intelligence and other technology tools take over jobs and cut off many traditional career paths.

item.title: AI Funding Boom Reaches Muni Market With Google-Tied Deal
item.description: Google parent Alphabet Inc. is poised to enter the municipal-bond market’s prepaid energy space by participating in a $1 billion transaction out of California, a major development in the evolution of a booming segment.

item.title: US Stock Futures Fall as US-Iran Flareup Threatens Peace Hopes
item.description: The rally in US stocks stalled before the bell on Wednesday as investors worried about the continued strain on a fragile ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran.

item.title: Options Traders Look Past Jobs Data as Inflation Takes Spotlight
item.description: Wall Street traders expect a strong jobs report on Friday. That may not be enough to soothe them, as the war-fueled oil price shock increasingly shifts the focus toward inflation.

item.title: Clayton Says Private Credit Can Do Better Job at Transparency
item.description: The New York City skyline Photographer: Jeenah Moon/Bloomberg
